skills.name: llama.cpp Local Server (Self-Hosted GGUF)
skills.tagline: Real OpenAI-compatible API on your own hardware. 108 t/s on GPU, 17 t/s on CPU. $0/month after install.
what_it_does: Installs llama.cpp (https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp, 90k+ stars, MIT) — provides llama-server and llama-cli
Downloads a Qwen 2.5 GGUF model from HuggingFace (1.5B Q8_0 for CPU, 3B Q4_K_M for GPU)
Launches an OpenAI-compatible API server on http://localhost:11434/v1 — drop-in replacement for OpenAI
GPU tier: qwen2.5-3b-instruct-q4_k_m.gguf, 108.7 tok/s, 0.24s wall time on a discrete GPU, ~2 GB VRAM
CPU tier: qwen2.5-1.5b-instruct-q8_0.gguf, 16.7 tok/s, 1.1–1.5s wall time on a modern x86_64 CPU, ~2 GB RAM
One-command start: llama-server -m model.gguf -c 2048 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 11434
Writes a verified install report with hardware tier, model, tok/s, and a sample prompt + response
use_cases: Privacy-first AI for sensitive business data — nothing leaves your box
Run AI offline during travel, remote work, or in the field
Slash AI costs from $100/month to $0 after the one-time install
Use the MacBook M2 you already own — llama.cpp has first-class Metal support
Run on any 4 GB+ GPU, any modern CPU, any Apple Silicon Mac
Drop-in OpenAI-compatible endpoint for any tool that already talks to OpenAI
skills.deliverable: llama-cpp-install.sh + download-model.sh + serve-llama-cpp.sh + verify-install.sh + .env.example + README
skills.who_its_for: Developers, privacy-conscious companies, hobbyists with a GPU, VPS owners, and anyone who refuses to send data to OpenAI
